items.reason: The logging guide replaces log_rate_limit with request/access/diagnostic sample rates and documents derivation and reject semantics.
The removed DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT environment variable is deleted from the client environment reference.
The dscli config table replaces log_rate_limit with the three new sample-rate flags and their derivation behavior.
The repository context index routes logging sampler questions to the new log-sampler design document.
The logging module overview adds LogSampler responsibilities and links to the new sampler design.
The new design document covers the LogSampler architecture, config rates, derivation, propagation, and hot-path constraints.
This existing logging design still documents log_rate_limit/DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT and client sync via log_rate_limit, but the PR removes that mechanism.
This trace context doc still says request sampling is created from local log_rate_limit, which is stale after the new request_sample_rate/LogSampler flow.
This PLOG design still frames slow logs as bypassing log_rate_limit sampling instead of the new diagnostic/request sampler semantics.
This implementation summary still references log_rate_limit request sampling and should be reconciled with the LogSampler behavior.
